title: Uniface Web Applications
text: Uniface Web Applications Uniface web applications run in a Uniface Web Application Server and are invoked by the client browser over the internet. To start a Uniface web application, the user uses a supported web browser to go to the URL of the main component. The URL specifies the web server and the requested component, which may be a Dynamic Server Page (DSP) or Static Server Page (USP). The user interface of these applications is provided by dynamic and/or static server page components, which generate and update HTML pages. For more information, see Dynamic Server Pages and Static Server Pages . Other application processing can be provided by service components that are activated by DSPs and USPs. Related Links Uniface Applications Uniface applications are typically mission-critical enterprise applications that use and maintain data stored in one or more databases, files, or other data resources. They may integrate with all kinds of non-Uniface applications, and they can be deployed on a wide variety of platforms. Web Application Server The Uniface Web Application Server (WASV) handles server-side processing for web and mobile applications. Uniface HTTP Processing HTTP is an internet protocol that provides a way for a client to send a request to a server and for the server to return a response. It is used in web applications, mobile apps, and web services to transfer data in a defined request-response cycle. This topic describes how HTTP is used in Uniface applications.
